name: HillaryClinton.com
subject: re: your spot at the first debate
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Hillary for America, a grassroots campaign of 2
body:
You could be at the first debate | Derek --

The biggest event of the campaign so far is upon us: The first presidential debate is just a few days away.

I am pumped to see Hillary take that stage and face off against Donald Trump -- and I'm pretty excited that you could be there, too.

**[Hillary needs her best supporters by her side at the big moment. Add your name now for the chance to see her go head-to-head with Trump on the debate stage for the first time.](https://links.hillaryclinton.com/ctt?kn=3&ms=MjY1NDIxNjMS1&r=MTA3MzIzMDg3NjIwS0&b=0&j=ODgyODE3ODM0S0&mt=1&rt=0)**

We have a pretty good idea of what to expect from Trump in this situation. We’ve seen what happens when moderators try to force him to defend his vision for the country.

He’ll try to bully his way out of important questions -- insulting people until the issue is buried under vitriol. If he gets really stuck, he’ll try to run out the clock with meaningless blather, expressed forcefully. If past is prologue, we bet he’ll take shots at Hillary (and this team) at every chance he gets -- talk over her when he can, or shout down her responses.

**Derek -- Hillary’s got this: She’s ready for Trump and his blustering debate style. Add your name for a chance to witness this landmark event and show Hillary you’ve got her back all the way to the finish line:** [](https://links.hillaryclinton.com/ctt?kn=17&ms=MjY1NDIxNjMS1&r=MTA3MzIzMDg3NjIwS0&b=0&j=ODgyODE3ODM0S0&mt=1&rt=0)
Let’s go,

Robby

Robby Mook
Campaign Manager
Hillary for America

|
**Chip in $1 to be automatically entered for the chance to win a trip to the first presidential debate:**

| [](https://links.hillaryclinton.com/ctt?kn=15&ms=MjY1NDIxNjMS1&r=MTA3MzIzMDg3NjIwS0&b=0&j=ODgyODE3ODM0S0&mt=1&rt=0)

---

**No purchase, payment, contribution or signing up to receive text messages is necessary to win, and will not improve chances of winning.**

Void where prohibited. Entries must be received between 12:00 a.m. (all times Eastern) on 9/16/16 and 11:59 p.m. on 9/19/16. Enter by visiting [here](https://links.hillaryclinton.com/ctt?kn=12&ms=MjY1NDIxNjMS1&r=MTA3MzIzMDg3NjIwS0&b=0&j=ODgyODE3ODM0S0&mt=1&rt=0) to donate to Hillary for America, or enter by visiting [here](https://links.hillaryclinton.com/ctt?kn=16&ms=MjY1NDIxNjMS1&r=MTA3MzIzMDg3NjIwS0&b=0&j=ODgyODE3ODM0S0&mt=1&rt=0) to sign up to volunteer for Hillary for America, or enter by texting DEBATE to 47246 to sign up to receive periodic text messages from Hillary for America. Msg & data rates apply. Text HELP for help & STOP to cancel. Visit [here](https://links.hillaryclinton.com/ctt?kn=4&ms=MjY1NDIxNjMS1&r=MTA3MzIzMDg3NjIwS0&b=0&j=ODgyODE3ODM0S0&mt=1&rt=0) to enter for free, without contributing, volunteering, or signing up to receive text messages. One (1) winner will receive an invitation for winner and one (1) guest to attend the Presidential Debate at Hofstra University on 9/26/16; round-trip airline tickets from within the fifty U.S. States, DC, or Puerto Rico to the New York area and one (1) night hotel accommodation (approx. value of Prizes: $1750). Odds of winning depend on number of entries received. Promotion open only to U.S. citizens or residents of the 50 states, DC or Puerto Rico and 18 or older (or age of majority under applicable law). All prizes awarded. Promotion subject to Official Rules [here](https://links.hillaryclinton.com/ctt?kn=5&ms=MjY1NDIxNjMS1&r=MTA3MzIzMDg3NjIwS0&b=0&j=ODgyODE3ODM0S0&mt=1&rt=0) and additional restrictions on eligibility. Sponsor: Hillary for America, 300 Cadman Plaza W, Brooklyn, NY 11201. | | **** | | Paid for by Hillary for America, a grassroots campaign of 2.3 million donors committed to electing Hillary Clinton (and keeping Donald Trump out of the White House).

name: Justice for Governor
subject: Thank goodness Cole didn't get his way
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Justice for Governor
body:
Justice Statement On Special Session

Today, businessman Jim Justice issued the following statement on the start the special session to address West Virginia’s flood recovery:

“Governor Tomblin was right to stop Bill Cole’s irresponsible plan to drain West Virginia’s Rainy Day fund during the special session the budget.

“The Rainy Day fund is for extreme emergencies like we saw this past June. Bill Cole wanted to raid the taxpayers’ savings account in order to sweep the budget crisis under the rug in an election year. That’s not leadership; that’s a typical politician running for the hills.

“Governor Tomblin did right by our people and protected our cash reserves. I hope this special session moves quickly and the recovery effort can continue without the politicians in Charleston slowing it down.”

subject: Friend, please read this
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Marco Rubio for Senate 2016
body:
This wasn't easy to sit down and write to you...
| [](http://paracom.paramountcommunication.com/ct/37654941:WD0lUdbCN:m:1:1328517760:7FC0D1EFBAA603F5142FF90821A7CDE2:r)
---
Friend,

This email is difficult to write. In fact, I have never written an email like this before.

I really hope you will take just a minute to read it.

**Today I'm going to ask you to make a personal commitment. And I hope and pray I can count on you.**

You see, if you are like me, you are probably very tired of this long campaign season - and the divisiveness and negativity this year has carried seems like it has reached an astounding all-time high.

But I'm sure you'll also agree with me that the stakes are so high in this election - for the future of our country - that we have to do everything we can to secure a Republican majority in the Senate and elect leaders who -- no matter the outcome of the presidential race -- will reverse Barack Obama's agenda.

**[BUT it all starts with your help today -- only with your IMMEDIATE action today can we ensure the United States Senate remains in Republican control.](http://paracom.paramountcommunication.com/ct/37654941:WD0lUdbCN:m:1:1328517760:7FC0D1EFBAA603F5142FF90821A7CDE2:r)**

That's why I made the decision -- a personal commitment to you and my fellow conservatives -- to run for re-election.

But before I ask you for your commitment in return, I'm going to lay out my own, very personal commitment to you.

First, my commitment to you:

1. **I will campaign as hard as I can until the very last vote is counted.** Every day I will wake up and fight for you and America to ensure liberal Democrats don't control the U.S. Senate.
2. **I will fight to reverse every destructive item of the Obama Agenda.** Barack Obama's presidency has been a disaster for America - and with your help I will reverse every last destructive policy.
3. **I will make the necessary sacrifices to win this race and defeat my liberal opponent, Patrick Murphy - a die-hard Obama apologist and Clinton supporter.** I know you and your family make sacrifices every day and I am committed to making the necessary sacrifices to win this race and serve our nation in the US Senate.
4. **My word is my bond, and I will keep every commitment I make to you.** We all know politicians who say one thing and do another. I will always tell you the truth and you can count on me to follow through with my commitments to you.

I hope you accept this, my promise and commitment to you.

**But in return, I am asking you to make a commitment to me.**

Friend, will you commit to helping me defeat my Democrat opponent, keep Republican control of the Senate, and ultimately ensure that I am able to fight for us AND reverse Barack Obama's disastrous policies?

| [** I'M COMMITTED >>> **](http://paracom.paramountcommunication.com/ct/37654941:WD0lUdbCN:m:1:1328517760:7FC0D1EFBAA603F5142FF90821A7CDE2:r)
---

**I need your commitment to win this race. I truly can't do it without you.**

Can I count on your commitment?

Because Friend, my campaign is facing critical fundraising challenges and frankly, I don't have anywhere else to turn. So, that's why I'm turning to you, and that's why your commitment in return is so important.

[** I'M COMMITTED >>> **](http://paracom.paramountcommunication.com/ct/37654941:WD0lUdbCN:m:1:1328517760:7FC0D1EFBAA603F5142FF90821A7CDE2:r)
---

**[I know I can't win this election without your support, so I hope I'll be able to count on you today. I want to personally thank you for reading my email and I'm hopeful you will make the necessary commitment to my campaign.](http://paracom.paramountcommunication.com/ct/37654941:WD0lUdbCN:m:1:1328517760:7FC0D1EFBAA603F5142FF90821A7CDE2:r) **

Marco Rubio

P.S. This election is not going to be easy, but our future is worth fighting for. I'm facing a tough campaign against Barack & Hillary's handpicked Democratic candidate. They are viewing his success as an extension of their own and are going to be working around the clock to see him win and me defeated. **[I need your help to fight back.](http://paracom.paramountcommunication.com/ct/37654941:WD0lUdbCN:m:1:1328517760:7FC0D1EFBAA603F5142FF90821A7CDE2:r)**

[** I'M COMMITTED >>> **](http://paracom.paramountcommunication.com/ct/37654941:WD0lUdbCN:m:1:1328517760:7FC0D1EFBAA603F5142FF90821A7CDE2:r)

| **For Immediate Release**
September 19, 2016

**Media Contact** : Ethan Latour
[ethan@philscott.org](mailto:ethan@philscott.org)
802-595-3776
**‘I will veto a new sales tax on services’**
_Phil Scott the only candidate focused on the economy and affordability_ Middlesex, Vt. – In response to his opponent’s statements supporting the creation of a new state sales tax on over 160 services, Phil Scott today said he would veto such a measure if it crossed his desk as Governor.

“My standard for evaluating public policy is very clear: If an idea decreases the costs of living and doing business here, I will support it. If it increases costs, I will oppose it. It’s that simple.” Scott said.

Scott noted that there are over 160 services that could be subjected to the new sales tax that his opponent supports, and that it would further disadvantage the local economies of communities along Vermont’s borders.

“Child care, cleaning services, accounting and bookkeeping, home heating maintenance and repair, barbers and hairdressers, website developers – the list goes on and on and on,” Scott said. “Add to this the $700 million in costs imposed on Vermonters by my opponent’s party over the last seven years – and the promise from some legislative leaders to advance a new ‘carbon tax’ on transportation and home heating fuels that could add as much as 88-cents to a gallon of gas – and you get a very clear picture of what is at stake in this election,” Scott said.

“We simply cannot make Vermont more affordable, or strengthen the economy, by continuing to increase the tax burden on families and employers. This is exactly the type of thinking that has created the Crisis of Affordability many Vermonters are confronting,” he added.

**_No Long-Term Reduction in the Tax Burden_**
"There is also no evidence to support the assertion new taxes on services would result in a reduction in the tax burden, or cost of living, over the mid-term or long-term," Scott continued. "Instead, all of the evidence I’ve seen – and this has been my experience in the Legislature – is that when state government increases the number of things being taxed, it uses that new capacity to steadily increase the overall tax burden over time.”

**_Requires Expanded State Bureaucracy _**
Expanding the sales tax to services would also require the state's tax department to add employees, management systems and enforcement officers.

"Dramatically expanding the scope of the sales tax would also require a large, and expensive, expansion of state government in order to administer it," Scott said. "These costs would also fall onto taxpayers and make balancing the budget in other pro-growth areas all the more challenging."

**_Voters Have a Clear Choice_**
"Vermonters have a clear choice," Scott concluded. “I will fight to make Vermont more affordable and our economy stronger, and my opponent is already pledging to support higher taxes on working families and businesses,” he said. “In my view, we need to be moving in the opposite direction -- toward lower tax burdens and lower costs of living -- if we want to strengthen Vermont’s economy and make our state more affordable. Increasing the number of things state government can tax is not the answer.”
**###**

clean_body: | | | | | | | | | | ---|--- | | | ---|--- | | | ---|--- | For Immediate Release September 19, 2016 Media Contact : Ethan Latour mailto:ethan@philscott.org 802-595-3776 ‘I will veto a new sales tax on services’ Phil Scott the only candidate focused on the economy and affordability Middlesex, Vt. – In response to his opponent’s statements supporting the creation of a new state sales tax on over 160 services, Phil Scott today said he would veto such a measure if it crossed his desk as Governor. “My standard for evaluating public policy is very clear: If an idea decreases the costs of living and doing business here, I will support it. If it increases costs, I will oppose it. It’s that simple.” Scott said. Scott noted that there are over 160 services that could be subjected to the new sales tax that his opponent supports, and that it would further disadvantage the local economies of communities along Vermont’s borders. “Child care, cleaning services, accounting and bookkeeping, home heating maintenance and repair, barbers and hairdressers, website developers – the list goes on and on and on,” Scott said. “Add to this the $700 million in costs imposed on Vermonters by my opponent’s party over the last seven years – and the promise from some legislative leaders to advance a new ‘carbon tax’ on transportation and home heating fuels that could add as much as 88-cents to a gallon of gas – and you get a very clear picture of what is at stake in this election,” Scott said. “We simply cannot make Vermont more affordable, or strengthen the economy, by continuing to increase the tax burden on families and employers. This is exactly the type of thinking that has created the Crisis of Affordability many Vermonters are confronting,” he added. No Long-Term Reduction in the Tax Burden "There is also no evidence to support the assertion new taxes on services would result in a reduction in the tax burden, or cost of living, over the mid-term or long-term," Scott continued. "Instead, all of the evidence I’ve seen – and this has been my experience in the Legislature – is that when state government increases the number of things being taxed, it uses that new capacity to steadily increase the overall tax burden over time.” Requires Expanded State Bureaucracy Expanding the sales tax to services would also require the state's tax department to add employees, management systems and enforcement officers. "Dramatically expanding the scope of the sales tax would also require a large, and expensive, expansion of state government in order to administer it," Scott said. "These costs would also fall onto taxpayers and make balancing the budget in other pro-growth areas all the more challenging." Voters Have a Clear Choice "Vermonters have a clear choice," Scott concluded. “I will fight to make Vermont more affordable and our economy stronger, and my opponent is already pledging to support higher taxes on working families and businesses,” he said. “In my view, we need to be moving in the opposite direction -- toward lower tax burdens and lower costs of living -- if we want to strengthen Vermont’s economy and make our state more affordable. Increasing the number of things state government can tax is not the answer.” | | | | | | | | | | | | Phil Scott for Governor, Glen Wright, Treasurer, 46 Three Mile Bridge Road, Middlesex, VT 05602 Want to change how you receive these emails? A new polling trend has emerged | Derek --

In the last week or so, a new polling trend has emerged: Trump’s path to the presidency is no longer a pipe dream -- it is clear and it is real.

Trump has more and more ways he can get to 270 electoral votes -- that should give all of us pause, Derek. **[Chip in for the first time today to help us stop him and reach our goal of 2.5 million donors by the end of the month -- we’ll send you a free sticker to say thanks.](https://links.hillaryclinton.com/ctt?kn=7&ms=MjY1NDg2NzES1&r=MTA3MzIzMDg3NjIwS0&b=0&j=ODgyOTQ2NzgyS0&mt=1&rt=0)**

He has made serious gains in states like New Hampshire and Colorado -- places where we once held double-digit advantages. And he’s now leading polling averages in several key states, including Florida and Ohio.

A month ago, Trump had maybe a 10 or 20 percent chance of winning this election. Today, in most models, his odds have risen to 30 or 40 percent.

I realize that some people might have a higher tolerance for risk than others. But a 40 percent chance of Donald Trump in the White House should be too high for anyone.

So all of us who care about the future of our country need to commit to do something about it right now.

**And the best thing you can do? Invest in this campaign so we can keep making our case to anyone who is still undecided in this election. So that we can put more organizers on the ground to register voters who share our values -- and make sure they cast their ballots on Election Day:** [](https://links.hillaryclinton.com/ctt?kn=6&ms=MjY1NDg2NzES1&r=MTA3MzIzMDg3NjIwS0&b=0&j=ODgyOTQ2NzgyS0&mt=1&rt=0)
Thank you,

Robby

name: Jay Inslee
subject: Thanks for stepping up
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jay Inslee for Washington
body:
We hit our fundraising target thanks to you.

Dear Friend,

Thank you for stepping up again.

We hit our mid-month fundraising goal because friends like you understand how much is at stake in this election.

I know you share my commitment to the progress we've made in Washington over the past four years. We must not go back. But with my opponent able to access large amounts of cash from his rich friends, your support right now means we're ready and able to fight back against his well-funded attacks.

That's why it's so important to have your support. And that's why I'm so grateful. Thank you for your support at this critical point in the election home stretch. Your confidence means the world to me and Trudi.

Very truly yours,

Jay Inslee

P.S. It's not too late to step up. I'm counting on your support -- so, I hope you can make a donation.

name: Peter Hanscom
subject: Update from the field (please read)
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the Evan Bayh Committee, 133 W
body:
| [ ](https://bayh.bsd.net/page/m/32a504ab/7be36b49/1e8bcc1f/78f86b75/2658885460/VEsH/ "http://www.evanbayhforindiana.com/")
---
**50 DAYS UNTIL THE ELECTION!
[Pitch in $5 now to support our winning effort >>](https://bayh.bsd.net/page/m/32a504ab/7be36b49/1e8bcc1f/78f86b74/2658885460/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/bayh-email?refcode=20160919nd&amount=10")**
Hi Friend,

I'm pleased to report that thanks to your support, our organizers and volunteers have hit the ground running and are spreading out all over Indiana to make sure voters are hearing Evan's message.

But I won't lie -- I'm nervous. We have just 50 days left till the big E-Day, and _CNN_ has reported that **the Koch brothers are scaling up a huge field campaign in Indiana.** That means we have to double our efforts on the ground -- where campaigns are won and lost. **[Can I count on your help?](https://bayh.bsd.net/page/m/32a504ab/7be36b49/1e8bcc1f/78f86b74/2658885460/VEsF/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/bayh-email?refcode=20160919nd&amount=10")**

With the Kochs on the ground in Indiana, I've been poring over maps and spreadsheets, trying to make sure we have all our bases covered for a massive Get Out The Vote effort.

Will we be able to get it done for Evan? Honestly, at this point, it's going to come down to resources. That's why I'm calling on every Evan Bayh supporter to dig deep and help us out --**the sooner the better.**

**[Pitch in $5 now to help us out-work the Kochs on the ground and elect Evan Bayh as Indiana's next senator.](https://bayh.bsd.net/page/m/32a504ab/7be36b49/1e8bcc1f/78f86b74/2658885460/VEsC/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/bayh-email?refcode=20160919nd&amount=10")**

Thanks a lot,

Peter Hanscom

subject: striking back against Toomey’s Wall Street allies
disclaimer_text: Paid For By Katie McGinty for Senate
body:
[](https://act.myngp.com/el/5481325086646468608/3774886201434046464?refcode=e20160919.3&refcode2=e20160919.3nd&amount=25)
---
"the concern is that GOP outside groups will swamp Democrats

name: John Gregg
subject: This shouldn't be a partisan issue
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Gregg for Indiana
body:
You can't talk about the economy without talking about our education system.
| [](https://act.myngp.com/el/-7838501292492847104/-3119727303991097344?utm_campaign=adv_jobred_&utm_medium=email&utm_source=gregg&ms=em16160919advjobredgqfuljg&refcode=em16160919advjobredgqfuljg)
---
Peter, You can't talk about the economy without talking about our education system. Unfortunately, some folks don't understand that. But I believe that putting more Hoosiers to work means preparing our kids and our neighbors for the jobs of today and the future. Today, I joined State Superintendent of Public Instruction Glenda Ritz to unveil our plans to increase workforce readiness. Now we need to build a groundswell of support to show our state's leaders that Hoosiers demand action. **[Will you join Glenda Ritz and me in calling on our state's leaders to make workforce readiness a priority? Sign the petition now. ](https://act.myngp.com/el/-7838501292492847104/-3119727303991097344?utm_campaign=adv_jobred_&utm_medium=email&utm_source=gregg&ms=em16160919advjobredgqfuljg&refcode=em16160919advjobredgqfuljg)** If we don't start fighting for investments in career counseling, education, and training today, it's going to be much harder to build the economy Indiana deserves. As Glenda Ritz, Christina Hale, and I talk to employers and business leaders on the campaign trail, workforce readiness is one of their top concerns. And it's not just current Indiana employers that can run into trouble finding qualified employees to fill jobs -- large employers may invest in other states rather than building new factories, offices, and facilities in Indiana. This shouldn't be a partisan issue. But out-of-touch politicians like my opponent -- who hasn't released a single policy plan or proposal yet -- still don't get it. **[If we're going to make workforce readiness a priority, it's on us to fight for it. Will you join me by signing my petition now?](https://act.myngp.com/el/-7838501292492847104/-3119727303991097344?utm_campaign=adv_jobred_&utm_medium=email&utm_source=gregg&ms=em16160919advjobredgqfuljg&refcode=em16160919advjobredgqfuljg)** Thanks for your support, John
| | [**Add your name ->**](https://act.myngp.com/el/-7838501292492847104/-3119727303991097344?utm_campaign=adv_jobred_&utm_medium=email&utm_source=gregg&ms=em16160919advjobredgqfuljg&refcode=em16160919advjobredgqfuljg)
---

[](http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/m/f7fd860/2a380d31/1122c64d/fda6e6/3969868097/VEsF/p/eyJlM3NnWVdKd1lXZGxJSDE5Ijoic2VwdGVtYmVyLW1hdGNoIiwiZTNzZ2NtVm1ZMjlrWlNCOWZRPT0iOiJlMjAxNjA5MTlfbWF0Y2gzX3YyIiwiZTNzZ1RGQkJRVkYxWlhKNVUzUnlhVzVuSUgxOSI6IjMsMjUsNTAsMTAwLDI1MCw1MDAifQ==/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/september-match?refcode=e20160919_match3_v2&amounts=3,25,50,100,250,500")
